Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=glycine + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = sarcosine + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:19937, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57305, ChEBI:CHEBI:57433, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789; EC=2.1.1.20; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:12859184, ECO:0000269|PubMed:3838667}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:19938; Evidence={ECO:0000305|PubMed:12859184};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catalyzes the methylation of glycine by using S-adenosylmethionine (AdoMet) to form N-methylglycine (sarcosine) with the concomitant production of S-adenosylhomocysteine (AdoHcy), a reaction regulated by the binding of 5-methyltetrahydrofolate. Possible crucial role in the regulation of tissue concentration of AdoMet and of metabolism of methionine. {ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:Q14749}.
